chain.thought: Explanation: Most teams either exclusively use the Reynard 98i chassis or do not use it at all. All American Racing is the only team listed with “reynard 98i\neagle 987,” meaning they both have the Reynard 98i and also use a different model, so they both have and do not have that model.

judge_thought: Explanation: The question is referring to a chassis model. Most teams list a single chassis (e.g., Reynard 98i, Swift 009.c, Penske PC27-98). The only team that lists both the common model Reynard 98i and a different model (Eagle 987) is All American Racing (rows 28 and 29 show "reynard 98i\neagle 987"). This means All American Racing both has that model (Reynard 98i) and also has entries that do not use that model (Eagle 987). No other team lists multiple chassis models.
